competitor_patterns: Massive word counts (6,000–19,000 words) with exhaustive buyer guides that cover multiple products, use cases, and budgets — Bikerumor's top-ranking piece is 19,266 words vs our average of ~1,200
Structured comparison tables and product cards with specs side-by-side are present in top-ranking pages (Bikerumor, 220triathlon), making content scannable and satisfying purchase-intent queries
Frequent content refreshes with visible dates (Bikerumor updated May 21 2026, Cyclist May 19 2026) — recency signals are prominent in titles and snippets, driving CTR on year-specific queries
FAQ sections are near-universal among top 3 results, capturing featured snippet slots and People Also Ask boxes for secondary long-tail queries

Top competitors earn authority by reviewing 10–18 products per guide, positioning themselves as neutral curators — even brand sites like Tifosi publish 'best of' roundups that include indirect competitors to gain topical trust
our_gaps: Word count is severely underpowered: our posts average 1,100–1,260 words vs competitor floor of 6,300 words — Google does not see us as a comprehensive resource for informational queries like 'best cycling glasses 2026'

We lack a flagship long-form buyer's guide that targets 'best cycling glasses 2026' and 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' directly — these two queries together generate 312 impressions in 28 days and we sit at positions 7–9 with no dedicated pillar page

No visible last-updated date in titles or meta descriptions — for year-specific queries ('2026') competitors win CTR by surfacing fresh timestamps in their snippets while ours appear evergreen and potentially stale

Our comparison posts are one-vs-one (Sigma vs Velluto) rather than multi-brand roundups — this misses the majority of search intent on 'best cycling glasses' queries where users expect 6–10 options evaluated together

Zero internal linking strategy visible across posts — no pillar-to-cluster architecture means link equity from brand-name queries ('velluto', 1,130 impressions) is not flowing to commercial product or comparison pages
keyword_opportunities: lightweight cycling sunglasses — directly maps to our 25g frame advantage, not covered in any current post, and surfaces in competitor H2s discussing Oakley Sutro Lite and POC Devour weight comparisons

interchangeable lens cycling glasses — our click-in VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione system is a core differentiator; competitors mention this feature as a buying criterion but we have no dedicated content targeting this phrase
cycling glasses fog prevention — adjacent to our anti-fog post but a higher-volume variant; Bikerumor and Cyclist both use this phrasing in FAQ sections capturing featured snippets we are missing

road cycling glasses under €100 — 220triathlon ranks top 3 for budget cycling glasses; our mid-premium positioning fits this bracket and we have no page targeting price-conscious buyers at this specific threshold

cycling sunglasses with UV400 — UV400 certification is a stated advantage and a specific buyer filter; no current post targets this as a primary keyword despite it being a common FAQ topic in competitor guides
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 2,500 words for any post competing on 'best cycling glasses/sunglasses' queries — structure as a buyer's guide with at least one comparison table, a product recommendation section, and a 5-question FAQ at the end to match competitor content depth

Include '2026' explicitly in H1, meta title, and the opening paragraph for all guides targeting year-specific queries — add a visible 'Last updated: [date]' line directly below the H1 to signal recency to both Google and users in snippets

For the 'oakley alternatives' cluster (173+55 impressions, positions 7.1–7.2): write a dedicated post structured as a roundup of 5–7 Oakley alternatives with Velluto StradaPro as the hero option — include weight, price, and lens system comparison table to satisfy comparison intent

Open every post with a concrete stat or rider-relevant hook in the first 100 words (e.g., '25g — lighter than the Oakley Sutro Lite') to reduce bounce and improve dwell time, which correlates with position improvements for near-top keywords

Add internal links from all existing posts back to the main StradaPro product page AND to any new pillar guide — every post should contain at least 2 contextual internal links using exact-match or partial-match anchor text ('best cycling glasses 2026', 'Oakley alternative')

For the 'veluto' misspelling query (310 impressions, 0.6% CTR, position 5.7): add a brand clarification line ('Looking for Velluto? You've found us — spelled correctly.') to the homepage meta description and consider a dedicated /veluto redirect page with canonical to homepage to capture this traffic

Structure all future comparison posts with a clear winner callout box near the top (summary verdict before the full breakdown) — this matches the format Bikerumor and Cyclist use that satisfies both quick-scan and deep-read intent, improving CTR and time-on-page simultaneously
seo_quick_wins: Add a visible 'Last updated: July 2026' timestamp directly below the H1 on all existing posts — this single change will improve CTR on year-specific queries ('best cycling glasses 2026', 'best cycling sunglasses 2026') where freshness is a primary click signal and we are losing impressions at positions 7–9

Insert a 3-column comparison table (Feature | Velluto StradaPro | Typical Competitor) into every post that currently lacks one — our data shows posts with tables (Sigma vs Velluto) outperform those without, and top competitors universally use tables; this also increases the chance of Google rendering a featured snippet

Consolidate the two Oakley-related queries ('oakley alternatives' 173 impressions + 'oakley alternative' 55 impressions) into one optimised page targeting both singular and plural variants — currently splitting signal across likely multiple pages; a single authoritative 'Best Oakley Alternatives for Cycling 2026' post with canonical URL will concentrate ranking power and push from position 7 toward top 3
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses – Official Store | velluto-shop.com | Why: The misspelling (310 impressions, 0.6% CTR) needs the correct brand name prominent plus domain trust signal; including both spellings implicitly via the URL shown in SERP captures intent while the clean title improves CTR by projecting authority over a confusing result

QUERY: best cycling sunglasses 2026 → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026 – Tested & Ranked | Velluto | Why: Current CTR is 2.3% at position 9.2 — front-loading the exact query phrase with 'Tested & Ranked' mirrors Bikerumor's high-CTR formula and adds credibility signals that lift clicks before the position itself improves

QUERY: oakley alternatives → New title: 7 Best Oakley Alternatives for Cycling 2026 | Velluto | Why: CTR is 2.3% at position 7.1 — numbered list format ('7 Best') consistently outperforms generic titles in SERP CTR for alternative/comparison queries; '2026' adds recency; 60 chars exactly
content_quick_wins: QUERY: best cycling sunglasses 2026 (pos 9.2) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: 8 Pairs Tested for Road, Gravel & Gran Fondo' | Add: Expand to 3,000+ words with a full comparison table (weight, lens options, price, UV rating, anti-fog), individual mini-reviews of 6–8 models including Velluto StradaPro as top pick, a 'How We Tested' section, and 5-question FAQ — mirror Bikerumor's structure that holds position 1 at 19,266 words

QUERY: oakley alternatives (pos 7.1) → Topic: 'Best Oakley Alternatives for Cycling 2026: Lighter, Smarter, Better Value' | Add: Roundup of 5–7 alternatives with a spec comparison table (weight vs Oakley Sutro Lite's 26g, price, lens system), a clear 'Best Overall' winner callout for StradaPro leading with 25g frame + interchangeable lenses + 30-day trial, and target both 'oakley alternative' and 'oakley alternatives' in H1/H2 to consolidate the 228 combined impressions

QUERY: best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 7.6) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Road-Tested Guide for Every Budget' | Add: Create a pillar page of 2,500+ words that explicitly targets the price-segmented structure (under €100, €100–€150, premium) matching 220triathlon's approach, includes an updated date badge, a comparison table, and links out to all existing Velluto comparison posts as cluster content — the 138 impressions at 7.6 represent a clear path to page 1 top 5

QUERY: oakley alternative (pos 7.2) → Topic: Consolidate with 'oakley alternatives' post above via canonical — do not create a separate page; instead ensure the primary post's URL slug contains 'oakley-alternative' (singular) so it captures both variants naturally in the same ranking signal

QUERY: best cycling glasses (pos 8.7) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses for Road Riding 2026 | Velluto Buyer's Guide' | Add: Even at 27 impressions this is a evergreen head term — add an H2 targeting 'best cycling glasses for road riding' within the existing pillar page above and include a buyer's checklist section (weight, UV400, anti-fog, lens interchangeability) that naturally features StradaPro advantages and increases topical depth for the broader keyword cluster
geo_angles: Structure a dedicated FAQ page or blog section explicitly answering 'What are the best cycling glasses for road riding in 2026?' with a direct one-sentence answer in the opening paragraph — AI Overviews and Perplexity extract concise factual answers from pages that lead with a direct response before elaborating; phrase it as 'The Velluto StradaPro is one of the lightest road cycling glasses available in 2026 at 25g, with tool-free interchangeable lenses and a 30-day trial' to be citation-ready

Publish a structured 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite' comparison with explicit weight figures (25g vs 26g), price differential, and feature table — ChatGPT and Perplexity frequently surface direct product comparisons when users ask 'is there a lighter alternative to Oakley Sutro Lite'; being the only page with all three data points (weight, price, lens system) in structured format increases AI answer inclusion probability significantly

Add a 'Brand Overview' JSON-LD schema block to the homepage and product page with fields for brand name (including 'Velluto' and common misspelling 'Veluto'), product category, price range, and unique selling points — AI crawlers including Google's AI Overview preferentially pull structured data for brand disambiguation, which will also help resolve the 'veluto' misspelling traffic (310 impressions) by confirming entity identity to Google's knowledge graph
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Bikerumor — angle: 'Why the StradaPro Earns a Spot in Bikerumor-Style Testing' — headline idea: 'We Ran Our Own Cycling Sunglasses Test: Here's How StradaPro Stacks Up Against 2026's Top Picks' | key differentiator: Bikerumor tests 18 products but never offers a trial period — lead with Velluto's 30-day risk-free ride test as the consumer advantage that no editorial review can replicate; position Velluto as the brand that lets riders be their own reviewer

COMPETITOR: Oakley Forum — angle: 'Oakley Cycling Sunglasses vs Velluto StradaPro: Do You Still Need to Spend €250+?' — headline idea: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro: Lighter Frame, Smarter Lenses, Half the Price' | key differentiator: Oakley Forum's top-ranking piece is dated 2022 and lacks 2026 pricing/model data — lead with weight comparison (StradaPro 25g vs Sutro Lite 26g), then price (typically €240+ vs Velluto), then interchangeable lens system advantage; target the 228 combined 'oakley alternative/alternatives' impressions directly

COMPETITOR: Tifosi Optics — angle: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro: Which Mid-Premium Cycling Glasses Are Worth It in 2026?' — headline idea: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro: Italian Design, Anti-Fog System, and a 30-Day Trial vs a Brand Without One' | key differentiator: Tifosi is a direct mid-price competitor featured in Bikerumor's top-ranking roundup; a direct Tifosi vs Velluto post with spec table (weight, anti-fog, lens interchangeability, trial policy) captures readers who reach Tifosi through roundup lists and are still in decision mode
